title: dodecatheon patulum var. typicum
intro: Dodecatheon patulum var. typicum, also known as western trickcampion, is a perennial plant species in the gentian family that grows in the western regions of North America. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as iridoids and phenolic acids. Western trickcampion has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue healing. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
